title: Redirect Semantics, Method Rewriting, Credential Scope, and Loop Control · Browser and Node… · Inference Brief: Sensitive Field Forwarding
summary: A sign-in flow follows relative and cross-origin redirects while Fetch redirect modes, credentials, methods, and authorization scope differ. Planned artifact: Add and verify the http ts sensitive field forwarding increment in a TypeScript 7 standards-based Fetch auditable redirect policy engine. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Strip or deliberately reissue authorization, cookies, origin-sensitive fields, and bodies when authority changes. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.

Bound redirect hops, detect loops, retain the chain, and expose the policy decision to callers. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
